Class BulletSystem

java.lang.Object
xyz.yaszu.freedom.Util.BulletSystem
All Implemented Interfaces:
org.bukkit.event.Listener
public class BulletSystem extends Object implements org.bukkit.event.Listener
A flexible and efficient bullet system for all types of guns. Supports configurable particles, damage, speed, charge mechanics, and callbacks. AMMO SYSTEM: - By default, all weapons share a single "base_ammo" pool per player (backwards compatible) - Call allocateWeaponId() or initializeAmmoPersistent() to give a weapon its own separate ammo pool - Weapons with their own IDs don't interfere with other weapons' ammo counts USAGE: 1. Basic (shared ammo): initializeAmmo(player, weapon, 30) 2. Persistent (unique ammo): initializeAmmoPersistent(player, weapon, 30)

    Method
    Description
    static void
    allocateWeaponId(org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack weapon)
    Allocates a unique persistent weapon ID for an item Call this if you want a weapon to have its own dedicated ammo pool without sharing the base ammo pool
    static boolean

    Checks if player can fire a specific weapon (has ammo and not reloading)

    initializeAmmo(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack weapon, int maxAmmo)
    Initializes the ammo system for a player's weapon If the weapon doesn't have a weapon_id, it will use the base ammo pool
    static void
    initializeAmmoPersistent(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack weapon, int maxAmmo)
    Initializes the ammo system with a persistent weapon ID This allocates a unique ID for the weapon and gives it its own ammo pool

  • Method Details

    • fireBullet

      public static org.bukkit.scheduler.BukkitRunnable fireBullet(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, BulletSystem.BulletConfig config)
      Fires a bullet from a player in the direction they're looking
      Parameters:
      player - The player firing the bullet
      config - The bullet configuration
      Returns:
      The BukkitRunnable task (already scheduled)

    • fireBullet

      public static org.bukkit.scheduler.BukkitRunnable fireBullet(org.bukkit.entity.Player firer, org.bukkit.Location startLocation, org.bukkit.util.Vector direction, BulletSystem.BulletConfig config)
      Fires a bullet from a specific location in a specific direction
      Parameters:
      firer - The player who fired the bullet (for damage and collision checks)
      startLocation - The starting location of the bullet
      direction - The direction the bullet travels
      config - The bullet configuration
      Returns:
      The BukkitRunnable task (already scheduled)

    • isCharging

      public static boolean isCharging(org.bukkit.entity.Player player)
      Checks if a player is currently charging a weapon
      Parameters:
      player - The player to check
      Returns:
      true if the player is charging, false otherwise

    • stopCharging

      public static void stopCharging(org.bukkit.entity.Player player)
      Stops charging for a player and removes all slowness effects
      Parameters:
      player - The player to stop charging

    • allocateWeaponId

      public static void allocateWeaponId(org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack weapon)
      Allocates a unique persistent weapon ID for an item Call this if you want a weapon to have its own dedicated ammo pool without sharing the base ammo pool
      Parameters:
      weapon - The weapon item to allocate an ID for

    • hasWeaponId

      public static boolean hasWeaponId(org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack weapon)
      Checks if a weapon has a persistent weapon ID
      Parameters:
      weapon - The weapon item
      Returns:
      true if the weapon has its own ID, false if using base ammo pool

    • getBaseAmmoKey

      public static String getBaseAmmoKey()
      Gets the base ammo pool key (all weapons without IDs share this)

    • initializeAmmo

      public static void initializeAmmo(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack weapon, int maxAmmo)
      Initializes the ammo system for a player's weapon If the weapon doesn't have a weapon_id, it will use the base ammo pool
      Parameters:
      player - The player
      weapon - The weapon item
      maxAmmo - The maximum ammo capacity

    • initializeAmmoPersistent

      public static void initializeAmmoPersistent(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack weapon, int maxAmmo)
      Initializes the ammo system with a persistent weapon ID This allocates a unique ID for the weapon and gives it its own ammo pool
      Parameters:
      player - The player
      weapon - The weapon item
      maxAmmo - The maximum ammo capacity
